evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: designing the data foundations that power real-time operational decisions
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD combines real-time delivery framing with explicit quality control/testing/monitoring responsibilities and company-expansion urgency, showing rough parity between velocity and rigour signals.
evidence.domain_risk: designing the data foundations that power real-time operational decisions
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Real-time operational decisions affect the business without explicit financial-reporting, regulatory, or compliance language, placing this at moderate risk.
evidence.data_team_maturity: establishing analytics engineering best practices during company expansion
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The mandate to establish best practices during company expansion signals a data function still being defined and built out, an early-stage signal despite an existing modern stack.
evidence.jd_authorship: owning data inputs for AI systems, implementing quality controls through testing and monitoring
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical framing around owning AI data inputs, real-time pipelines, and named tools (BigQuery, dbt, Dagster/Airflow) shows someone who has done this job wrote it.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: constructing core data models for analytics and AI workflows
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the data/AI infrastructure layer itself rather than a specific commercial, product, or finance function, making this internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: establishing analytics engineering best practices during company expansion
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role is explicitly tasked with establishing best practices and owning foundational data architecture during expansion, a strategic-direction mandate rather than scoped execution.
evidence.ai_role: owning data inputs for AI systems
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to build and own data infrastructure that AI systems consume, making them an enabler rather than a user of AI coding tools.
evidence.testing_framing: implementing quality controls through testing and monitoring
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Quality controls via testing and monitoring are framed as an owned responsibility using an explicit implementation verb, making this a responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: implementing quality controls through testing and monitoring
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Quality controls and monitoring are a named responsibility reflecting operational reliability concern for real-time systems, secondary to the primary foundation-building mandate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: designing the data foundations that power real-time operational decisions
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Designing foundational data models and establishing best practices during company expansion is explicitly greenfield build work.
evidence.loss_aversion: implementing quality controls through testing and monitoring — reflects operational concern about pipeline reliability rather than compliance.
